title: Glandularia ciliata
taxonomy: Glandularia ciliata is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The classification of Glandularia ciliata includes its kingdom as Plantae and its kingdom rank as Angiosperm. It belongs to the order Lamiales and the family Verbenaceae. This plant is further classified as a genus Glandularia and species G. ciliata.
uses: Glandularia ciliata, also known as the Chilean pink verbena, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Glandularia ciliata is also said to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of the plant's medicinal uses.
benefits: Glandularia ciliata is known to have various health benefits due to its rich composition of bioactive compounds. parts: Glandularia ciliata, commonly known as pink skullcap, is a plant used in traditional medicine. The medicinal parts of Glandularia ciliata include its flowers, leaves, and roots. preparations: Glandularia ciliata, also known as purple fringe, is a species of flowering plant that has been used in herbal preparations for various purposes.
